channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fuel prices fall sharply after tax cuts and Cyclone Maila monitored near Queensland
item.description: This week in Australia, petrol prices across all major cities have dropped significantly, with reductions of around 31 to 35 cents per litre following federal and state government fuel tax cuts. The f...

item.title: GSIS to Refund Over P19 Billion to Members Under Balik Ginhawa Program
item.description: The Government Service Insurance System (GSIS) announced it will refund more than P19 billion in loan payments to approximately 1.37 million members and pensioners through its Balik Ginhawa program. U...

item.title: TFF Confirms Damaro’s Eligibility, Dismissing Simba’s Protest
item.description: Dar es Salaam – The Tanzania Football Federation (TFF) has officially dismissed Simba SC’s protest regarding the eligibility of Young Africans SC’s midfielder, Mohammed Damaro Camara. Simba had argued...

item.title: Commission of Inquiry set to release findings on 2025 election violence
item.description: The Commission of Inquiry investigating the violence surrounding Tanzania's October 2025 General Election is about to submit its findings. President Samia Suluhu Hassan formed the nine-member commissi...

item.title: Canadian Passport Applications to Receive Refund if Processing Exceeds 30 Days
item.description: Starting April 1, 2026, Canadian passport applicants are eligible for a full refund if their application takes longer than 30 business days to process. The federal government, through Immigration, Ref...

item.title: IMF urges Pakistan to address fuel price distortions despite subsidy measures
item.description: The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has advised Pakistan to remove distortions in petroleum pricing as soon as possible, despite acknowledging the Rs152 billion subsidy extended by the federal gover...

item.title: UK Government Details Near-Activations of Emergency Alert System
item.description: The UK government has disclosed several incidents that nearly warranted the activation of the national emergency alert system, which sends messages and sirens to mobile phones to warn of life-threaten...

item.title: PNP vows to prosecute individuals spreading false energy news amid fuel price hikes
item.description: The Philippine National Police (PNP) has declared its commitment to help the national government combat disinformation regarding the energy sector, particularly during the current series of oil price ...

item.title: Gujarat High Court issues policy restricting AI in judicial processes
item.description: The Gujarat High Court has formalised a policy strictly prohibiting the use of artificial intelligence in any judicial decision-making processes. This includes drafting orders, preparing judgements, d...

item.title: Pakistan Experiences Rising Temperatures Exceeding 2030 Climate Projections
item.description: According to the Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD), Pakistan is experiencing unusually high temperatures that are already exceeding thresholds set for 2030, despite contributing minimally to gl...

item.title: Joburg Officials’ High Salaries Amid Persistent Service Delivery Failures
item.description: Residents of Johannesburg have valid concerns over the substantial salaries awarded to the heads of the city’s 13 service entities, which range from R1 million to almost R5 million annually. These ent...

item.title: Dar City aim for playoff qualification in BAL clash with Petro de Luanda
item.description: Dar es Salaam’s Dar City basketball team is set for an important BAL match against Angola’s Petro de Luanda at the SunBet Arena in Pretoria, South Africa. The game, tipping off at 8pm East African tim...

item.title: Concerns rise over misuse of blue badge parking permits in England
item.description: Councils across England have been urged to tackle both legitimate and fraudulent misuse of blue badge parking permits, following reports that one in 15 adults now holds one. Blue badges are issued to ...

item.title: NDPC Investigates Remita and Sterling Bank Over Potential Customer Data Breach
item.description: The Nigeria Data Protection Commission (NDPC) don start investigation over matter wey concern Remita Payment Services Ltd and Sterling Bank. The probe, wey begin on April 1, 2026, dey focus on whether...

item.title: Police report on early morning Hermès Brisbane ram-raid
item.description: In the early hours of Easter Monday, a luxury Hermès store in Brisbane's CBD was targeted in a ram-raid using a stolen white Mercedes SUV. The incident occurred shortly after 3am at the corner of Edwa...

item.title: Kenya’s Oil Procurement Crisis and Accountability Challenges
item.description: Kenya is facing a brewing controversy over oil procurement that has led to the resignation of several high-ranking officials, including a principal secretary and two CEOs of state corporations. Allega...

item.title: India engages diplomatically with Qatar and UAE amid West Asia tensions
item.description: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar held separate phone conversations with Qatari Prime Minister Sheikh Mohammed bin Abdulrahman bin Jassim Al Thani and UAE Foreign Minister Sheikh Abdullah bin Za...

item.title: Cape Town Urges Residents to Save Water Amid Declining Dam Levels
item.description: The City of Cape Town has issued a warning to residents to conserve water as dam storage levels have dropped to 47.8%. Despite a slight reduction in daily consumption, usage remains above the city's t...

item.title: Chocolate Bars Shrink and Flavors Change Despite Falling Cocoa Prices
item.description: Even though cocoa prices have dropped to a near three-year low, chocolate-flavored bars and smaller pack sizes are not likely to revert to their original formulations. Many popular bars, such as Toffe...

item.title: Tanzania Prepares National Swimming Team for Africa Championships in Algeria
item.description: Tanzania's national swimming team is gearing up for the 2026 Africa Aquatics Swimming Championships, which will now take place in Oran, Algeria, from May 5 to 10, after a last-minute change of host co...
